title: Scientists Find a Protein That Can Cause Blindness in Diabetes
summary: Scientists have discovered a protein called LRG1 that can cause eye problems in people with diabetes. This eye disease is called diabetic retinopathy and can lead to blindness. The protein makes tiny blood vessels in the eye squeeze too tight, which stops oxygen from reaching the retina. This starts a chain reaction that damages vision over time. In tests with diabetic mice, blocking LRG1 stopped the early eye damage and kept their eyes healthy. This finding could help doctors create new treatments to prevent blindness before it starts, instead of just fixing damage after it happens. The research was done by scientists at UCL and supported by charities like Diabetes UK.

a health condition where blood sugar is too high
the part at the back of the eye that helps you see
a way to help sick people get better

background_read: Diabetes is a common health problem where people have too much sugar in their blood. Over time, this can damage parts of the body, like the eyes. Diabetic retinopathy is an eye disease that happens when high blood sugar hurts the tiny blood vessels in the retina, which is the part of the eye that lets you see. This can lead to vision loss or blindness. Scientists are always looking for new ways to help people with diabetes keep their eyes healthy. In this article, they found a protein that starts the eye damage early, which might lead to better treatments to stop blindness before it happens.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists at UCL and supported by Diabetes UK
WHAT: Discovered a protein called LRG1 that causes early eye damage in diabetes
WHERE: In the eye's retina, tested in mice
WHY: To find new ways to prevent blindness in people with diabetes

perspectives.description: This discovery offers hope for preventing blindness in millions with diabetes by targeting LRG1 early.
